description: Extend the shared relationship authorization plan contracts so they can represent People relationship subjects without introducing endpoint execution behavior.
acceptance-criteria: Relationship auth specs can carry person-specific subject metadata for Student, Contact, and Staff in addition to existing EducationOrganization subjects.

Person auth object metadata includes the selected auth view/table name, expected person DocumentId output column, claim EducationOrganizationId column, direct-claim-match flag set to false, and auth failure hint text.
Person subject metadata can represent direct root-column bindings, transitive ordered join paths, and zero-hop self-person root DocumentId bindings.
Stored and proposed anchor metadata are explicit enough for later GET-many and CRUD SQL builders to consume without re-resolving strategy-specific behavior.
Existing EdOrg-only relationship contract behavior, including AllowDirectClaimMatch for EdOrg hierarchy checks, remains compatible with current consumers.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationContracts.cs for People-capable contract fields and unchanged EdOrg semantics.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter

description: Teach the shared People core to classify the five People-involved relationship strategies as supported core strategies with explicit subject-kind eligibility.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople selects EducationOrganization, Student, Contact, and Staff subjects.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted selects EducationOrganization, Student, Contact, and Staff subjects while marking only EdOrg hierarchy checks as inverted.
RelationshipsWithPeopleOnly selects Student, Contact, and Staff subjects and excludes EducationOrganization subjects.
RelationshipsWithStudentsOnly selects Student subjects only.
RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility selects Student subjects only and records responsibility-based auth view selection.
Duplicate configured relationship strategies are preserved as separate OR entries with their raw configured indexes and relationship-local order.
NamespaceBased, OwnershipBased, custom view strategies, NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired, and invalid strategy classification behavior remain unchanged outside this People core scope.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.cs for People strategy support and unchanged non-People classifications.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier
Add or update unit tests that assert the subject-kind matrix for all five People strategies.

description: Implement People auth view selection and missing-view configuration failure metadata from the existing auth object definitions.
acceptance-criteria: Student subjects use auth.EducationOrganizationIdToStudentDocumentId with Student_DocumentId.
Contact subjects use auth.EducationOrganizationIdToContactDocumentId with Contact_DocumentId.
Staff subjects use auth.EducationOrganizationIdToStaffDocumentId with Staff_DocumentId.
RelationshipsWithStudentsOnlyThroughResponsibility uses auth.EducationOrganizationIdToStudentDocumentIdThroughResponsibility with Student_DocumentId.
Each selected People auth view carries the matching auth.md hint text.

When a selected, operation-applicable person subject requires People auth views that were not emitted, the planning result is a security-configuration failure that names the resource, strategy, selected person kind, auth view, and all missing resources from AuthObjectDefinitions.RequiredPeopleAuthAssociationResourceNames.

Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies that select only applicable EducationOrganization subjects continue with those EdOrg subjects even when AuthObjectDefinitions.HasAllPeopleAuthViewAssociations is false.
The planner does not emit executable specs that reference conventional People auth view names when AuthObjectDefinitions.HasAllPeopleAuthViewAssociations is false.
steps-to-verify: Review the People auth view mapping against src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/AuthObjectDefinitions.cs and AuthEdOrgHierarchyContracts.cs.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner
Review planner unit tests for all four auth view selections, hint metadata, missing-association security-configuration failures, and EdOrg-only continuation when People views are suppressed.

description: Implement People subject selection from resolved securable element paths, including direct person references, transitive person references, and self-person resources.
acceptance-criteria: Direct Student, Contact, and Staff securable elements resolve to the person DocumentId column on the subject resource root table or proposed root row.
Transitive Student, Contact, and Staff securable elements retain the ordered ResolveSecurableElementColumnPath hop chain through intermediate resource tables.
Student, Contact, and Staff resources with their own person securable element produce a zero-hop self-person subject whose terminal value is the aggregate root DocumentId.
No People subject uses UniqueId or USI values as the authorization value.
One People authorization subject is produced per independent declared securable element path, with dedupe limited to identical canonical physical predicate keys.
Contributor metadata preserves original JSON path, readable or MetaEd name, person kind, and contribution order for every contributing person securable element.
steps-to-verify: Review the new People selector against src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/AuthSecurableElementContracts.cs and the mapping set securable column path contracts.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector
Add unit tests covering a direct Student reference, a transitive CourseTranscript-to-Student path, Contact and Staff references, and zero-hop Student/Contact/Staff self subjects.

description: Apply ODS-parity subject scope filtering and predicate dedupe rules for People relationship subjects.
acceptance-criteria: Person securable paths whose resolved path starts from a child collection table are excluded from executable People subjects.
Excluded child-collection People paths are retained as skipped or ineligible contributor metadata with reason ChildCollectionPersonPathOutsideSubjectScope.

Identical People predicates are deduped only within a single configured strategy/check using a key that includes physical person path, value anchor, terminal person kind, auth view, and output column.
Duplicate configured strategies remain separate OR entries even when their selected People predicates are identical.
Normal and inverted mixed strategies are not deduped together, and responsibility-based Student subjects are not deduped with non-responsibility Student subjects.
If ODS subject-scope filtering leaves a selected strategy with no executable subjects, the result is a no-applicable-subject security-configuration failure that includes skipped-path diagnostics.
steps-to-verify: Review the People predicate key and skipped-path metadata implementation for strategy-local scope.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector
Add unit tests for child-collection exclusion, same-path dedupe with merged contributors, duplicate strategy preservation, and no-applicable-subject failures.

description: Integrate selected People subjects with the existing relationship authorization planner while preserving EdOrg behavior for mixed strategies.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeople combines applicable root-table EducationOrganization subjects and applicable People subjects with AND semantics inside each strategy.
RelationshipsWithEdOrgsAndPeopleInverted preserves the EdOrg direct-claim-match flag and swaps only the EdOrg hierarchy Source/Target filtering metadata.
Person auth view semantics are the same for normal and inverted mixed strategies.
Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies continue with applicable People subjects when EdOrg securable elements resolve only to non-root paths.
Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies continue with applicable EducationOrganization subjects when no selected, operation-applicable People subject requires a People auth view.
A true unresolved configured securable element remains a security-configuration failure with resource, strategy, and securable element details.
Multiple configured relationship strategies preserve OR composition metadata, raw configured indexes, and relationship-local ordering.
No endpoint adapter or SQL compiler is wired to execute People relationship checks in this task.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner.cs for combined EdOrg and People planning.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner
Add unit tests for mixed EdOrg-and-People, inverted mixed strategy metadata, People-only strategies, and unresolved element failures.

description: Model stored-value, proposed-value, and create-new eligibility for People subjects so later CRUD execution can consume the core without inventing parallel rules.
acceptance-criteria: Stored People specs carry root-table stored anchors and the ordered person path needed to resolve null or broken stored transitive paths.
Proposed People specs carry explicit proposed root-row or first-hop anchors derived from the ResourceWritePlan, with direct references represented as the degenerate single-anchor case.

Zero-hop self-person subjects are marked ineligible for POST create-new with reason SelfPersonDocumentIdUnavailableForCreateNew, while remaining applicable for stored and existing-resource operations.

When a self-person subject is ineligible for create-new and another subject in the same strategy is executable, the ineligible subject is omitted from that operation's AND check and retained in eligibility metadata.
When every subject in a selected strategy is operation-ineligible for create-new, the planner exposes a no-executable-subjects security-configuration failure with diagnostic eligibility reasons.
The core does not create a same-row People bootstrap subject for primary association resources whose proposed row would only become visible in a selected People auth view after insertion.
steps-to-verify: Review proposed anchor metadata against src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.External/Plans/WritePlanContracts.cs.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner

Add unit tests for stored transitive anchors, proposed direct and transitive anchors, self-person create-new ineligibility, mixed executable/ineligible strategy handling, and all-ineligible strategy failures.

description: Extend relationship authorization failure metadata and mapping inputs so People failures can be rendered later by AUTH1 and ProblemDetails consumers.
acceptance-criteria: People failure metadata includes person kind, selected auth view/table name, expected person DocumentId output column, value source, configured strategy identity, relationship-local order, and contributing securable element metadata.
No-claims metadata for People relationship checks identifies the selected person auth view and hint so GET-many can short-circuit to an empty result and CRUD can map to 403 later.

Stored null or broken transitive person paths are represented as stored-value invalid-data metadata for the original person securable element, not as security configuration or no-relationship metadata.
The metadata distinguishes GET-many treatment of null or broken stored person paths as nonmatching rows from CRUD stored-value invalid-data mapping for a specific resource.
Missing proposed person path values are represented as proposed element-required metadata for the original person securable element.
The existing versioned compact AUTH1 failure-set ordinal model can map People strategy and subject ordinals back to the extended metadata without adding user-readable values to the payload.
Security-configuration failures for no applicable People subjects include resource, strategy, selected subject kinds, and skipped or unresolved securable element details.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload.cs and RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.cs for People metadata compatibility.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper
Add unit tests for People no-claims, stored invalid-data metadata, proposed missing metadata, auth-view hints, and ordinal-to-metadata mapping.

description: Preserve the existing staged runtime behavior for GET-many and CRUD adapters until DMS-1095 and DMS-1158 consume the People core.
acceptance-criteria: Existing GET-many adapters do not expose or execute People relationship specs before the DMS-1095 integration story.
Existing GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE adapters do not expose or execute People relationship specs before the DMS-1158 integration story.
Known-but-not-enabled or explicit staging behavior remains in place for endpoint paths that are not part of this core story.
EdOrg-only GET-many and CRUD authorization behavior remains unchanged.
No E2E feature tags are re-enabled as part of this core story.
No production DDL, auth view definitions, or database execution paths are changed except where needed to inspect existing auth object metadata.
steps-to-verify: Review PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter, SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler, and CRUD orchestrators for unchanged People execution staging.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor
Search the E2E feature files to confirm no @relational-backend tags were re-enabled for People scenarios in this story.

description: Add focused unit coverage for the People relationship core and run the scoped formatting and test pass for the implementation.
acceptance-criteria: Unit tests cover strategy classification for all five People relationship strategies.

Unit tests cover subject-kind selection, auth view selection, inverted EdOrg metadata, transitive person path handling, zero-hop self subjects, responsibility-based Student handling, and no-subject security failures.
Unit tests cover missing People auth view guard behavior, child-collection path exclusion, operation eligibility reasons, contributor metadata, and duplicate strategy preservation.
Existing EdOrg-only planner, adapter, AUTH1 payload, and failure mapper tests still pass.
Changed C# files under the DMS backend and backend unit test projects are formatted with csharpier.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor
Run git diff -- tasks.json and confirm no production or test source files were edited by the task-refinement pass itself.

description: Medium: Preserve security-configuration precedence before returning People endpoint staging failures.
acceptance-criteria: GET-many, GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE relationship authorization paths do not short-circuit to a People not-implemented response before relationship planning has identified true security-configuration failures.

When effective authorization contains both a People relationship strategy and invalid or unresolved security metadata, the endpoint returns the existing security-configuration failure surface instead of a 501 not-implemented response.

When effective authorization contains People relationship strategies but no security-configuration failures, the endpoint continues to return the staged not-implemented response until DMS-1095 or DMS-1158 consumes the People core.
Known-but-not-enabled strategy diagnostics are not lost when People staging remains the final response.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationalDocumentStoreRepository People staging guard placement against RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner security-configuration precedence.
Add focused repository unit coverage for a People relationship strategy combined with an invalid strategy name and verify the security-configuration result wins.
Add focused repository unit coverage for a People relationship strategy without security-configuration failures and verify the staged not-implemented result remains.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalDocumentStoreRepository

description: Low: Remove the unused People auth view selector or wire it into the planner if it is still needed.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationPeopleAuthViewSelector and RelationshipAuthorizationSelectedPeopleAuthView are either removed or have a real production call site.
People auth view selection has a single authoritative implementation that cannot drift from RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector and RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner.
No tests depend on a dead helper that production planning never uses.
steps-to-verify: Run rg "RelationshipAuthorizationPeopleAuthViewSelector|RelationshipAuthorizationSelectedPeopleAuthView" src/dms and confirm there are no unused production-only definitions.
Review People auth view selection paths in RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner and RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector for duplicated logic.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner

description: Medium: Preserve People DocumentId path and anchor metadata when mapping relationship failures to the external DTO.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationPersonSubjectInfo, or an equivalent external DTO shape, includes the person subject path kind and ordered DocumentId path steps for direct, transitive, and self-person subjects.
Mapped People failure DTOs preserve proposed anchor metadata when it exists, including whether the anchor is the root row or first hop.
Transitive People failures no longer expose only the terminal intermediate table and column as a misleading root binding.
Existing EdOrg-only relationship failure DTO behavior remains unchanged.
Unit coverage proves direct, transitive, and self-person failure mapping preserve person kind, auth view, hint, DocumentId path, and proposed anchor metadata.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External/Backend/RelationshipAuthorizationFailure.cs for People path and anchor fields on the external failure DTO.

Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.cs to confirm PersonMetadata.Path and ProposedAnchor are mapped without re-resolving securable elements.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload

description: Medium: Keep People-core-only planning failures behind endpoint staging until DMS-1095 and DMS-1158 consume the core.
acceptance-criteria: GET-many, GET-by-id, POST, PUT, and DELETE continue returning the known-but-not-enabled People staging response when the only security-configuration failures are produced by selected People subjects, such as missing People auth view associations, no applicable People subjects, or no executable People subjects for the current operation.
Invalid or unresolved non-People security metadata still takes precedence over People staging as already required.

The relationship authorization planner still reports People-core security-configuration failures for core/unit consumers; only the current endpoint adapters keep those failures behind the staged endpoint response.
Mixed EdOrg-and-People strategies do not execute partial People specs or leak MissingPeopleAuthViewAssociations responses before DMS-1095 or DMS-1158 consumes endpoint execution.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationalDocumentStoreRepository People staging flow around PlanStoredValues, PlanUpdateValues, and security-configuration failure handling.
Add focused repository unit coverage for People missing-auth-view and no-applicable-subject planning failures and verify the endpoint result remains the known-but-not-enabled staging response.
Add focused repository unit coverage for People strategies combined with invalid or unresolved non-People security metadata and verify the security-configuration result still wins over staging.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalDocumentStoreRepository

description: Low: Centralize People relationship strategy staging classification and relationship-local order calculation.
acceptance-criteria: People relationship strategy detection for endpoint staging reuses a shared helper or classifier-owned metadata instead of duplicating a strategy-name switch in RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.
Known-but-not-enabled People staging failures calculate relationship-local order with the same NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired skip rule used by RelationshipAuthorizationStrategyClassifier.
Raw configured strategy indexes remain preserved on ConfiguredAuthorizationStrategy diagnostics.
Adding or renaming a People relationship strategy requires updating one authoritative classification path for both planning and endpoint staging.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.TryCreatePeopleEndpointStagingFailures and RelationshipAuthorizationEndpointExecutionBoundary for duplicate People strategy switches.
Add focused unit coverage where NoFurtherAuthorizationRequired precedes a People strategy and verify the staging failure relationship-local order matches classifier ordering.

description: Low: Collapse duplicated MissingPeopleAuthViewAssociations failure construction in the relationship authorization planner.
acceptance-criteria: The two CreateMissingPeopleAuthViewAssociationFailures call paths share one failure-construction helper after selecting People auth views.
Stored-value, proposed-value, and update-plan value-source behavior remains unchanged.
MissingPeopleAuthViewAssociations diagnostics continue to include resource, strategy, relationship-local order, auth object, location, hint, person metadata, and contributors.
No behavior changes are made to EdOrg-only or mixed-strategy planning beyond removing duplicated construction logic.

description: Medium: Format People relationship security-configuration failures with People-specific wording instead of EdOrg-only diagnostics.
acceptance-criteria: Security-configuration messages for People UnresolvedSecurableElement failures identify Student, Contact, or Staff securable elements and do not say the strategy requires resolvable EducationOrganization elements.

Security-configuration messages for child-collection or no-applicable People subject failures identify the selected People subject kind, skipped path reason, auth view when available, and contributor metadata.
Security-configuration messages for People MissingProposedRootBinding failures do not describe the missing binding as an EducationOrganization subject.
The EdOrg-only grouped security-configuration formatter is used only when every grouped failure is for an EducationOrganization subject.
Existing EdOrg-only security-configuration wording and tests remain unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.HasOnlyEdOrgSubjectSelectionFailures and BuildSecurityConfigurationFailureMessage for People-aware branching by failure.Location.Kind and PersonMetadata.

Add focused repository unit coverage for People unresolved, child-collection no-applicable, and missing proposed binding failures and assert the returned security-configuration errors use People-specific wording.

Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs

description: Move executable relationship authorization auth object ownership from check specs and People metadata onto each relationship authorization subject.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationSubject has a required non-null RelationshipAuthorizationAuthObject value and continues to expose IsPersonSubject from PersonMetadata.
RelationshipAuthorizationCheckSpec no longer has an AuthObject property or constructor argument.
RelationshipAuthorizationPersonSubjectMetadata no longer has an AuthObject property or constructor argument.
RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMetadata continues to allow auth object metadata for planning and configuration failures that occur before an executable subject exists.
All production and test call sites that construct relationship authorization subjects, check specs, or People metadata compile against the new contract shape.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationContracts.cs for the new subject-owned auth object contract.
Run rg "checkSpec\.AuthObject|CreateCheckSpecAuthObject|PersonMetadata\?\.AuthObject|personMetadata\.AuthObject" src/dms and confirm no stale executable-subject auth object reads remain.

description: Update EdOrg and People relationship subject selection so every executable subject is stamped with the auth object required to evaluate that subject.
acceptance-criteria: RelationalEdOrgAuthorizationSubjectSelector selects subjects for one SupportedRelationshipAuthorizationStrategy and stamps each EdOrg subject with the direction-specific EdOrg hierarchy auth object.
RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector stamps each Student, Contact, Staff, and responsibility Student subject with the selected People auth object on RelationshipAuthorizationSubject.
People skipped contributors and pre-subject failure metadata retain auth object metadata only where needed for diagnostics before an executable subject exists.
Mixed EdOrg plus People strategies can produce subjects with distinct auth objects without collapsing them to one strategy-level auth object.
People-only strategies with more than one person kind can produce distinct subject auth objects in the same configured strategy.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationalEdOrgAuthorizationSubjectSelector.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationalPeopleAuthorizationSubjectSelector.cs.
Add or update unit tests for normal EdOrg, inverted EdOrg, mixed EdOrg plus Student, and People-only Student plus Contact subject auth object stamping.

description: Simplify relationship authorization planner check-spec creation and no-claims failure construction around subject-level auth objects.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner no longer creates or stores a check-spec-level auth object for stored-value or proposed-value checks.
ApplyProposedAnchorToPersonSubject preserves subject.AuthObject while replacing only the People proposed anchor metadata.
No-claims failure metadata is derived from distinct subject auth objects, producing one metadata entry per needed auth object while preserving the configured strategy identity.
Pre-subject configuration failures continue to derive auth object metadata from the selected eligible subject when no executable subject exists.
Relationship OR metadata, configured strategy indexes, relationship-local order, and ineligible subject metadata are unchanged by the refactor.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationPlanner.cs for removal of CreateCheckSpecAuthObject and check-spec auth object constructor usage.

description: Update the single-record relationship authorization SQL compiler to read auth object metadata from the subject being emitted.
acceptance-criteria: SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationSqlCompiler reads the current subject's auth object when emitting direct-claim-match and auth object EXISTS predicates.
SQL compiler helper methods that need auth-object behavior accept an explicit RelationshipAuthorizationAuthObject argument instead of reading a check-spec-level auth object.
Existing EdOrg-only single-record authorization SQL output remains unchanged for PostgreSQL and SQL Server.
Compiler tests and fixtures construct RelationshipAuthorizationSubject instances with auth objects and no longer depend on RelationshipAuthorizationCheckSpec.AuthObject.
A mixed-auth-object check spec is not rejected by the compiler model merely because the check spec has no top-level auth object; endpoint support remains enforced by the execution boundary.

acceptance-criteria: PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter selects exactly one EdOrg auth object from check-spec subjects after the current execution boundary has accepted the plan.
PageDocumentIdAuthorizationSpecAdapter fails clearly when a page-document check contains multiple distinct auth objects or a non-EdOrg auth object.
RelationshipAuthorizationEndpointExecutionBoundary validates endpoint support by inspecting all subject auth objects rather than a check-spec-level auth object.
Unsupported auth object messages identify the first unsupported subject auth object.
People GET-many and CRUD endpoint execution remains staged until DMS-1095 and DMS-1158 consume the People core.

description: Update the external relationship authorization failure DTO so failed subjects carry auth object detail and failed strategies retain only a homogeneous summary.
acceptance-criteria: RelationshipAuthorizationFailedSubject has a required non-null RelationshipAuthorizationAuthObjectInfo value.
RelationshipAuthorizationPersonSubjectInfo no longer duplicates auth object metadata.
RelationshipAuthorizationFailedStrategy.AuthObject remains nullable and is documented by usage as a summary for homogeneous failed subjects.
The final DTO shape does not leave a nullable or optional subject-level auth object path that mapper code can silently omit.
Core, backend, and handler tests that construct relationship authorization failure DTOs compile against the new shape.
Existing EdOrg-only response payload semantics remain unchanged except for the additional per-subject auth object detail.

description: Refactor relationship authorization failure mapping to use subject-level auth objects for AUTH1 runtime failures and no-claims failures.
acceptance-criteria: Runtime AUTH1 mapping populates each failed subject's external auth object from RelationshipAuthorizationSubject.AuthObject.
Runtime AUTH1 strategy-level AuthObject is selected from only the failed subject ordinals represented in the AUTH1 payload.
Runtime AUTH1 strategy-level AuthObject is null when failed subjects in the same configured strategy have distinct auth objects.
No-claims mapping maps all executable subjects for the failed configured strategy and uses null strategy summary for mixed-auth-object failures.
No-claims mapping matches no-claims failure metadata back to subjects by subject auth object, and by People kind/path when that metadata is available.
People hints, person kind, DocumentId path, stored anchor, and proposed anchor metadata remain available after auth object duplication is removed from People subject info.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper.cs for removal of check-spec auth object fallback logic.
Add or update tests for mixed EdOrg plus People failures where only the People subject fails, both subjects fail, and no claims are present.
Add or update tests for People-only Student plus Contact failures and homogeneous Students-only failures.
Add or update tests proving mixed EdOrg plus Student no-claims failures produce one failed strategy with a null strategy auth-object summary and per-subject auth objects.

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMapper|RelationshipAuthorizationAuth1FailurePayload"

description: Update repository staging and relationship authorization error formatting to consume subject-level failure auth objects without changing endpoint behavior.
acceptance-criteria: RelationalDocumentStoreRepository no longer reads People auth object metadata from PersonMetadata for executable-subject failures.
Repository staging helpers prefer failed subject auth object metadata when a subject exists and continue to use RelationshipAuthorizationFailureMetadata.AuthObject for pre-subject planning failures.
Known-but-not-enabled People staging behavior and security-configuration precedence remain unchanged.
Mixed-auth-object failed strategies do not invent one user-facing auth object name when the strategy summary auth object is null.
Existing EdOrg-only security-configuration and authorization message tests remain semantically unchanged.
steps-to-verify: Review src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationalDocumentStoreRepository.cs and src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend/RelationshipAuthorizationErrorMessageFormatter.cs.
Add or update repository tests for People staging failures, mixed-auth-object failures, and EdOrg-only failure message regressions.
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter RelationalDocumentStoreRepository

description: Complete the focused test and formatting pass for the auth-object ownership refactor.
acceptance-criteria: Planner tests assert EdOrg normal and inverted subjects, People subjects, mixed EdOrg plus People subjects, proposed-value transformed subjects, and self-person create-new ineligible subjects preserve subject-level auth objects.
SQL compiler and adapter tests prove existing EdOrg SQL and page-document behavior remain unchanged after removing check-spec auth objects.
Failure mapper tests cover homogeneous and mixed-auth-object strategy summaries for AUTH1 and no-claims paths.
Core and backend handler tests prove relationship failure DTO construction and user-facing response semantics still work with subject-level auth object detail.
All touched C# files are formatted with csharpier and the final implementation diff contains no out-of-scope DDL, endpoint-enablement, or unrelated refactoring changes.
steps-to-verify: Run dotnet csharpier format src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.External src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit
Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit.csproj

Run dotnet test src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "RelationalDocumentStoreRepository|SingleRecordRelationshipAuthorizationExecutor|DefaultRelationalWriteExecutor"

Run dotnet test src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit.csproj --filter "GetByIdHandlerTests|DeleteByIdHandlerTests|UpsertHandlerTests|UpdateByIdHandlerTests"
Run git diff --stat and review the changed file list for out-of-scope production, test, DDL, or endpoint-enablement changes
